## 1. RENT AND FINANCIAL OBLIGATIONS

### 1.1 Rent Division

| Roommate | Room/Area | Monthly Share | Due Date |
|----------|-----------|---------------|----------|
| [ROOMMATE A] | [ROOM DESCRIPTION] | $[AMOUNT] | [DAY OF MONTH] |

**Total Security Deposit:** $[TOTAL DEPOSIT]

| Roommate | Deposit Share |

| [ROOMMATE B] | $[AMOUNT] |

### 1.3 Utilities
**Utilities Split Method (select one):**
- [ ] Equal split among all roommates
- [ ] Proportional to room size
- [ ] Custom split as specified below

| Utility | Responsible Party | Split Method |
|---------|------------------|--------------|
| Electricity | [NAME ON ACCOUNT] | [SPLIT DETAILS] |
| Gas/Heat | [NAME ON ACCOUNT] | [SPLIT DETAILS] |
| Water | [NAME ON ACCOUNT] | [SPLIT DETAILS] |
| Internet | [NAME ON ACCOUNT] | [SPLIT DETAILS] |
| Trash | [NAME ON ACCOUNT] | [SPLIT DETAILS] |

### 1.4 Late Payment
Rent payments not received by the [NUMBER]th day of the month will incur a late fee of $[AMOUNT] per day, up to a maximum of $[MAX AMOUNT]. If a roommate is more than [NUMBER] days late on two or more occasions, the other roommates may initiate the move-out provisions in Section 7.

---

## 2. SHARED SPACES AND PRIVATE AREAS

### 2.1 Private Spaces
Each roommate's bedroom is their private space. No roommate shall enter another's private space without explicit permission.

### 2.2 Shared Spaces
The following areas are shared and all roommates are equally responsible for their upkeep: [KITCHEN, LIVING ROOM, BATHROOMS, LAUNDRY AREA, BALCONY/PATIO, GARAGE, OTHER].

### 2.3 Storage
Each roommate is allocated the following storage space:
- [ROOMMATE A]: [STORAGE ALLOCATION]
- [ROOMMATE B]: [STORAGE ALLOCATION]

Common storage areas shall not be monopolized by any single roommate.

---

## 3. HOUSEHOLD RESPONSIBILITIES

### 3.1 Cleaning Schedule
**Cleaning approach (select one):**
- [ ] Rotating weekly schedule
- [ ] Each roommate responsible for specific areas
- [ ] Shared cleaning service at $[COST] per [FREQUENCY], split equally

**Cleaning Standards:**
- Kitchen: dishes washed within [NUMBER] hours of use, counters wiped daily, deep clean weekly
- Bathroom: cleaned [FREQUENCY]
- Common areas: tidied [FREQUENCY]
- Trash: taken out when full; [ROOMMATE/ROTATION] is responsible for bringing bins to curb

### 3.2 Groceries and Shared Supplies
- [ ] Each roommate buys their own groceries (separate shelves/areas)
- [ ] Shared grocery fund of $[AMOUNT] per person per month
- [ ] Hybrid: personal groceries separate, shared staples split equally

### 3.3 Appliances and Shared Property
Shared appliances and furniture are cataloged below. Damage caused by a specific roommate is that roommate's financial responsibility.

| Item | Owner | Condition at Move-In |
|------|-------|---------------------|
| [ITEM 1] | [OWNER] | [CONDITION] |
| [ITEM 2] | [OWNER] | [CONDITION] |

---

## 4. GUEST POLICY

a) **Overnight guests** are permitted up to [NUMBER] nights per week without prior approval. Guests staying more than [NUMBER] consecutive nights require written consent from all roommates.

b) **Extended guests** (more than [NUMBER] nights in any 30-day period) must be approved by all roommates and may be required to contribute to utilities.

c) **Parties and gatherings** of more than [NUMBER] people require at least [NUMBER] hours advance notice to all roommates.

d) Guests must adhere to all house rules. The hosting roommate is responsible for their guest's behavior and any damage caused.

e) No guest may be given keys, access codes, or entry devices without unanimous roommate approval.

---

## 5. NOISE AND QUIET HOURS

a) **Quiet hours**: [START TIME, e.g., 10:00 PM] to [END TIME, e.g., 8:00 AM] on weeknights (Sunday-Thursday) and [START TIME] to [END TIME] on weekends (Friday-Saturday).

b) During quiet hours, noise shall be kept to a level that does not disturb other roommates. This includes music, television, phone calls, video games, and conversations.

c) Extended noise (parties, gatherings, loud projects) during non-quiet hours requires [NUMBER] hours advance notice to all roommates.

---

## 6. SUBSTANCES AND PROHIBITED ITEMS

a) **Smoking/vaping** inside the residence: [PERMITTED IN DESIGNATED AREA / PROHIBITED ENTIRELY]

b) **Alcohol**: [PERMITTED / RESTRICTED TO PERSONAL ROOMS / OTHER]

c) **Controlled substances**: No illegal substances shall be used, stored, or distributed on the premises. Violation is grounds for immediate move-out proceedings.

d) **Pets**: [NO PETS PERMITTED / PETS PERMITTED WITH SPECIFICATIONS: SPECIES, SIZE LIMITS, DEPOSIT REQUIRED]

e) **Weapons**: [NO WEAPONS / SECURED STORAGE ONLY / OTHER POLICY]

---

## 7. MOVE-OUT AND TERMINATION

### 7.1 Voluntary Move-Out
A roommate wishing to move out must provide at least [NUMBER: e.g., 30, 60] days written notice to all other roommates and the landlord.

### 7.2 Replacement Roommate
The departing roommate may propose a replacement, subject to:
- Approval by all remaining roommates
- Landlord approval (if required by the primary lease)
- The replacement signing this Roommate Agreement

### 7.3 Involuntary Move-Out
A roommate may be asked to leave upon agreement of [MAJORITY / ALL] remaining roommates for:
- Repeated failure to pay rent (more than [NUMBER] late payments)
- Violation of substance policies
- Behavior that creates an unsafe living environment
- Material breach of this Agreement not cured within [NUMBER] days of written notice

### 7.4 Security Deposit Return
The departing roommate's share of the security deposit will be returned per the terms of the primary lease and applicable law, minus deductions for any damage attributable to that roommate.

---

## 8. COMMUNICATION

a) All formal notices under this Agreement shall be provided in writing (text message, email, or written note).

b) Household meetings may be called by any roommate with [NUMBER] hours notice. All roommates are expected to attend or provide input in writing.

c) Disagreements shall be resolved through good-faith discussion. If unresolved, roommates agree to seek mediation before pursuing legal remedies.

---

## BOTH PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE

By signing below, all roommates confirm that they have:

1. Read and understood all terms of this Agreement
2. Agreed to the financial, guest, noise, and household responsibility provisions
3. Entered into this Agreement voluntarily
4. Acknowledged the move-out and termination procedures
5. Verified their identity through biometric capture

## LEGAL DISCLAIMER

**THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR LEGAL ADVICE.** This roommate agreement is provided as a general template for shared living arrangements. It does not supersede or replace a primary lease agreement with a landlord. Enforceability varies by jurisdiction. All parties are encouraged to consult with a licensed attorney regarding their rights and obligations under applicable law.
